title: Research data supporting 'EEG recording during playing MOBA game'
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 23 participants during resting state (eyes closed and open) and active gameplay in a multiplayer online battle arena (MOBA) game. The study captures neural activity associated with gaming performance, including event markers for in-game actions such as kills, deaths, and match outcomes. Data were collected between April and July 2023 using a 64-channel Neuroscan system.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Neuroscan system with 64 electrodes at a sampling frequency of 1000 Hz. Recordings included resting state conditions (eyes closed and eyes open) and continuous EEG during MOBA gameplay. Event markers were coded to identify specific in-game events: kills (13), deaths (14), game start (66), game failure (444), and game victory (666).

funding_references.funder_name: This work was supported by a grant from the Chongqing Medical Youth Top Talent Project in Chongqing Municipal Health Commission (DDZ).

funding_references.award_title: Chongqing Medical Youth Top Talent Project
